name: remove-ai-slops
tagline: Agent workflow skill that strips AI-generated code smells from a branch diff via test-locked, phased cleanup with parallel background sub-ag
verdict: No secret access, network calls, or hidden instructions found - safe from malicious intent. Two real risks: Phase 6 can run `git checkout` on a file to revert a failed edit, which would also discard any of the user's own uncommitted changes to that file without asking first; and the skill assumes non-standard orchestration primitives (background `task()` age

highlights.summary: Phase 6 step 3: '`git checkout` the affected file (or use `git diff` + targeted `Edit` to revert just the problematic hunk).' This can discard the user's own uncommitted edits to that file if it overl
No explicit 'do NOT use for / when' guidance anywhere in the description or body, so a caller has no stated boundary for over-triggering this skill on non-cleanup tasks.
